title_en: Salah in Nayl al-Awtar: Prayer and Its Hadith Foundations
title_ar: Salah in Nayl al-Awtar: Prayer and Its Hadith Foundations
content_en:
Ash-Shawkani's prayer chapters in Nayl al-Awtar present the fullest available treatment of the hadith evidence for prayer law, organized by topic and combined with comparative analysis of how the four schools have interpreted the same texts. For the student who wishes to understand not just the rulings but their evidential basis, these chapters are invaluable.

The foundational hadiths for prayer times receive careful analysis. The hadith of 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr in which the Prophet described each prayer time window (Muslim, Abu Dawud) is assessed as sahih and taken as the primary evidence for the standard prayer time structure. The hadith of Jibril (in which the Angel Gabriel led the Prophet in prayer at the beginning and end times of each prayer) is treated as corroborating evidence. Ash-Shawkani notes that the two hadiths are mutually supporting and that the four schools' prayer time structures, while differing in some details, all derive from this same body of evidence.

On the obligation of al-Fatihah in every rak'ah, ash-Shawkani presents the strong hadith evidence: 'There is no prayer for one who does not recite the Opening of the Book' (al-Bukhari, Muslim) and 'Whoever prays a prayer and does not recite the Opening of the Book in it, it is deficient' (Muslim). He concludes that al-Fatihah is obligatory in every rak'ah and that the follower behind an imam must recite it as well — the hadith 'Whoever has an imam, the imam's recitation is his recitation' is assessed as weak and therefore cannot override the stronger evidence requiring individual recitation of al-Fatihah.

The chapter on the placement of hands in prayer addresses one of the school's most well-known differences. The hadiths supporting placing the right hand over the left on the chest in standing prayer (reported by Wa'il ibn Hujr and others in Abu Dawud, Ibn Hibban) are assessed as authentic. The practice of leaving the hands at the sides (the standard Maliki practice in Medina) is supported by fewer explicit hadiths and may reflect the Medinan tradition rather than an explicit prophetic instruction. Ash-Shawkani's assessment supports hand-placement over the chest or belly, with either position being valid.

The Amin (saying 'Amin' loudly after al-Fatihah) receives treatment with ash-Shawkani's characteristic attention to the hadith evidence. The hadith 'When the imam says Amin, say Amin' (al-Bukhari, Muslim) and the hadiths describing the Prophet and Companions saying Amin loudly in prayer (Abu Dawud, Ibn Majah) are assessed as sufficient to establish that saying Amin aloud in congregational prayer is sunnah — a position shared by the Shafi'i and Hanbali schools against the standard Maliki and Hanafi practice of saying it silently.

Ash-Shawkani's treatment of the qunut supplication in Fajr prayer presents the hadith evidence for and against it. He notes that Anas ibn Malik reported the Prophet performing qunut in Fajr until the end of his life (al-Bayhaqi), while other hadiths suggest qunut was specific to the 'qunut of calamity' (qunut an-nawazil) during a period of crisis. He assesses the evidence as genuinely contested and presents the scholarly positions without definitively resolving which is more strongly established — a display of intellectual honesty uncommon in partisan legal works.
title_id: Salat dalam Nayl al-Awtar: Fondasi Hadis untuk Ibadah Salat
content_id:
Bab-bab salat karya asy-Syawkani dalam Nayl al-Awtar menyajikan uraian paling lengkap yang tersedia tentang bukti hadis untuk hukum salat, disusun berdasarkan topik dan dikombinasikan dengan analisis komparatif tentang bagaimana mazhab empat menafsirkan teks yang sama. Bagi pelajar yang ingin memahami bukan hanya hukumnya tetapi dasar evidensialnya, bab-bab ini sangat berharga.

Hadis-hadis pokok tentang waktu salat mendapat analisis yang cermat. Hadis 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr di mana Nabi ﷺ menggambarkan jendela waktu setiap salat (Muslim, Abu Dawud) dinilai sebagai shahih dan dijadikan bukti utama untuk struktur waktu salat standar. Hadis Jibril (di mana Malaikat Jibril memimpin Nabi ﷺ salat pada awal dan akhir waktu setiap salat) diperlakukan sebagai bukti penguat. Asy-Syawkani mencatat bahwa kedua hadis itu saling mendukung dan bahwa struktur waktu salat mazhab empat, meskipun berbeda dalam beberapa detail, semuanya berasal dari kumpulan bukti yang sama.

Tentang kewajiban al-Fatihah di setiap rakaat, asy-Syawkani menyajikan bukti hadis yang kuat: 'Tidak ada salat bagi orang yang tidak membaca Pembukaan Kitab' (al-Bukhari, Muslim) dan 'Barangsiapa salat dan tidak membaca Pembukaan Kitab di dalamnya, maka itu kurang' (Muslim). Beliau menyimpulkan bahwa al-Fatihah wajib di setiap rakaat dan bahwa makmum di belakang imam pun harus membacanya — hadis 'Barangsiapa memiliki imam, maka bacaan imam adalah bacaannya' dinilai lemah dan karena itu tidak dapat mengesampingkan bukti yang lebih kuat yang mewajibkan bacaan al-Fatihah secara individual.

Bab tentang posisi tangan dalam salat membahas salah satu perbedaan mazhab yang paling terkenal. Hadis-hadis yang mendukung meletakkan tangan kanan di atas tangan kiri di dada saat berdiri dalam salat (diriwayatkan dari Wa'il ibn Hujr dan lainnya dalam Abu Dawud, Ibn Hibban) dinilai shahih. Kebiasaan membiarkan tangan di samping tubuh (praktik standar Maliki di Madinah) dibahas dengan cermat, dan asy-Syawkani menyimpulkan bahwa bukti untuk menaruh tangan di dada lebih kuat.
content_ar: هذا الفصل من الكتاب يتناول موضوعات إسلامية مهمة في إطار منهج أهل السنة والجماعة.
